title: Decoder does not validate geographic coordinate ranges
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 250-256

The _decode_pli() function decodes lat/lon microdegrees from binary but does not validate they fall within valid geographic ranges:
- Latitude: [-90, 90] degrees -> [-90000000, 90000000] microdegrees
- Longitude: [-180, 180] degrees -> [-180000000, 180000000] microdegrees

The XML parser DOES validate these ranges (lines 753-756), but the binary decoder does not.

Impact: A malformed or malicious compact CoT message could contain out-of-range coordinates that would:
1. Produce semantically invalid position data
2. Be accepted silently and propagated to ATAK systems
3. Potentially cause issues in downstream consumers expecting valid coordinates

Fix: Add validation after decoding:
```python
if not (-90_000_000 <= lat <= 90_000_000):
    raise DecodeError(f'Latitude {lat} out of range')
if not (-180_000_000 <= lon <= 180_000_000):
    raise DecodeError(f'Longitude {lon} out of range')
```
